site.title: Margin Notes
site.description: Quiet writing on craft, code, and the everyday.

newsletter.title: A quiet letter, now and then.
newsletter.description: Occasional notes from the margins: new essays, things worth reading, and the small observations that do not fit anywhere else.

posts.title: Writing in the Margins
posts.excerpt: On keeping a notebook of half-thoughts, and why the best ideas often arrive sideways.
posts.content:
There is a particular kind of quiet that arrives only after the keyboard stops. The cursor blinks, but it is no longer waiting. It is keeping you company.

## On craft

Most of the work that matters happens in the editing. The first draft is just a permission slip — it tells you that the idea exists, that it can survive a sentence.

> Write drunk on the idea. Edit sober on the reader.

## Small habits

I keep a folder of unfinished posts. Some of them are years old. Occasionally one of them turns into something. Usually they just remind me what I was thinking about that month.

### A short list

- Write the title last.
- Read the post out loud before publishing.
- Cut the first paragraph.

## Closing

If you made it this far, thank you. The internet is loud, and your attention is a gift.

posts.title: The Quiet Web
posts.excerpt: A love letter to small, slow, hand-made websites — and the people who still build them.
posts.content:
There is still a quiet web underneath the loud one. Hand-coded pages, weird sidebars, footnotes that lead somewhere personal.

## On craft

The small site is a craft object. It does not have to scale. It just has to be true.

## Small habits

View source. Save the page. Send a kind email to the author. The web rewards small attentions.

posts.title: Color & Tokens — A Reference
posts.excerpt: A living reference for the design system: background, foreground, primary, accent, muted, and the rule that ties them.
posts.content:
The palette here is small on purpose. Six roles, two themes, no exceptions. ## Surfaces The surfaces are the quiet half of the system — the paper the writing sits on.
Background--background
Foreground--foreground
Muted--muted
## Ink and accent Primary carries headings and emphasis; accent is the only color allowed to raise its voice.
Primary--primary
Accent--accent
Rule--border
## Using the tokens Reach for a role, not a hex. `bg-card` follows the theme; `bg-white` does not.

posts.title: Notes on Typography
posts.excerpt: What I've learned about line height, measure, and the quiet tyranny of the default font stack.
posts.content:
## Measure

A line of body text is most readable somewhere between 60 and 75 characters. Wider, and the eye loses the return; narrower, and the rhythm fragments.

## Leading

Generous leading is the easiest gift you can give a long page of prose.

## Closing

Defaults are a design decision. Choose them on purpose.

posts.title: Small Tools, Big Leverage
posts.excerpt: A handful of tiny scripts that quietly run my week. None of them are clever. All of them are mine.
posts.content:
## On craft

The best tool is the one you wrote yesterday for a problem you have today.

```sh
alias todo='vim ~/notes/today.md'
```

## Closing

Keep them small. Replace them often.

posts.title: Reading List: Winter
posts.excerpt: Books, essays, and a few longreads that kept me company through the cold months.
posts.content:
## Books

- *The Dispossessed* — Le Guin
- *Ways of Seeing* — Berger
- *A Field Guide to Getting Lost* — Solnit

## Essays

A handful of pieces that stayed with me longer than they should have.

posts.title: The Walk
posts.excerpt: Twenty minutes a day, no headphones. The smallest practice that changed the most.
posts.content:
## The pace

A walk is a thinking machine you wear with your shoes.

## The rule

No headphones. No phone. The point is the boredom; the boredom is the point.

posts.title: CSS I Keep Relearning
posts.excerpt: Margin collapse, stacking contexts, and the four properties I have to look up every single time.
posts.content:
## Margin collapse

Adjacent vertical margins collapse. It will surprise you again next month.

## Stacking contexts

`position: relative` does not create one. `z-index` on a non-positioned element does nothing.

## The four

`min-width: 0`, `flex-shrink`, `align-items: stretch`, and `overflow: clip`. I will look these up forever.

pages.title: About
pages.excerpt: About Margin Notes and Lee Park.
pages.content:
Hi, I'm Lee Park. Margin Notes is a small corner of the internet for slow thoughts on craft, code, and the everyday.

## Why this journal

A commonplace book in public — a place to keep the sentences that have stayed with me, and to think slowly in a year that mostly rewards speed.

## How it's made

Written in plain Markdown. Published when the sentence feels finished.

pages.title: Contact
pages.content:
The slowest way to reach me is also the most reliable: write to **[lee@example.com](mailto:lee@example.com)**.

For short notes, leave a comment on a post.

For production use, connect this page to your preferred contact workflow.

pages.title: Cookie Policy
pages.content: Margin Notes uses one functional cookie: a `localStorage` entry to remember your preferred color theme. We do not use analytics or tracking cookies.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.content: Margin Notes does not collect personal data. Comments are stored locally in your browser for demo purposes only.

pages.title: Terms of Service
pages.excerpt: Terms of service for Margin Notes.
